fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Maine Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Maine - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity maine, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to maine, respiratory therapist compact maine, maine respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: Maine Board of Respiratory Care Practitioners
board.parent_dept: Maine Office of Professional and Occupational Regulation (OPOR)
board.mailing_address:
Board of Respiratory Care Practitioners
35 State House Station
Augusta, ME 04333-0035

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination
quick_facts.ce_requirement: 30 contact hours of continuing respiratory care education during each two-year licensing period

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Maine.

steps.title: Register or Log In to MyLicense.Maine.Gov
steps.description: Access the MyLicense.Maine.Gov portal. If you are a new user, create an account; otherwise, log in with your existing credentials.

steps.title: Start New Application
steps.description: Once logged in, click on 'Apply for a New License' and select 'Respiratory Care Practitioner' from the list of professions to begin the specific application process.

steps.title: Complete Online Application Form
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the online application form on MyLicense.Maine.Gov, providing accurate personal, educational, and professional history details.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Upload or arrange for official submission of all necessary documents, such as official transcripts, NBRC credentials, and any other required forms, as specified within the MyLicense.Maine.Gov application instructions.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Remit the non-refundable application fee of $60 through the MyLicense.Maine.Gov portal using a credit or debit card.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check your application status within your MyLicense.Maine.Gov account and respond promptly to any requests for additional information or clarification from the Maine Board of Respiratory Care Practitioners.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Maine respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Maine have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Maine. To work in Maine, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Maine?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Maine while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Maine
hero.dek: To apply for a Respiratory Care Practitioner license in Maine, you must register or log in to the MyLicense.Maine.Gov online application system and complete the online application.

variants.label: Renewal

official_links.registration_steps: Create an account on MyLicense.Maine.Gov if you don't have one. Once logged in, navigate to 'Apply for a New License' and select 'Respiratory Care Practitioner' from the board/profession dropdown to start your application.
